description_long: This object is a traditional wooden Wing Chun dummy, known as a Muk Yan Jong, used for martial arts training. It features a central cylindrical trunk made of dark-stained wood, supported by a sturdy wooden frame with two vertical posts and horizontal crossbars. Three protruding wooden arms are attached to the front of the trunk at varying heights and angles, simulating an opponent's limbs, while a single angled leg extends from the lower section. The wood grain is visible throughout, giving it a natural, slightly rough appearance. The construction is robust, designed to withstand repeated strikes, and the overall finish is matte with no visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A robust, dark wooden Wing Chun dummy with three arms and one leg, mounted on a sturdy frame. The wood grain and matte finish give it a traditional, natural look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical trunk, three protruding arms, and the single angled leg, all supported by a rectangular wooden frame.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical trunk's depth, the horizontal extension of the arms, and the angled leg projecting forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the trunk and the supporting frame, with no arms or leg visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arms and leg extending outward, with the frame and trunk profile visible.
